Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick proposes his boldest surmise yet: that the 1974 intervention was a "surgical" re-editing of history that nullified the "evil tyrant" Nixon across all linear time (past, present, and future). He views himself as a "cuckoo's egg"—a time-traveler from AD 70 whose authentic personality is "Thomas." The Black Iron Prison, once a supreme temporal reality, was "winked out" in 1974, leaving a "vast silence" as the world awaits the arrival of the rightful King.

Key claims from this passage:
- Historic Re-editing: God bypassed natural causal laws to "overrule" and re-edit the past, making it so Nixon was never the tyrant he once was on "Track A."
- The Cuckoo’s Egg: PKD is not his real self; "Thomas" is a secret Christian time-traveler from AD 70, and "PKD" is a memory-less mask.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies his Bantam novels (*Ubik*, *Maze*, *Tears*) as vehicles of revelation and "scripture," arguing that *Ubik* contains an original cosmology that explains its own source. He reflects on the "Form axis" and "orthogonal time" from *Ubik* as keys to understanding how 1974 USA was actually Rome A.D. 70. He declares that the secret Christian Church finally conquered "Rome" (Nixon) in 1974, and the "King" (Zebra) is now here in discorporate form awaiting his final return.

Key claims from this passage:
- Ubik-as-Scripture: The novel *Ubik* was effectively written *by* the entity Ubik through automatic writing, making it a form of revelation.
- Conquest of Rome: Unlike Constantine's era, 1974 was the "genuine" conquest of Rome by the secret church, creating a vacuum for the King.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick outlines a three-stage progression of the human experience with the Divine: 1) Suffering/Dread (The world is real and hostile), 2) Amazement/Pantheism (The world is alive and animate), and 3) Bliss/Acosmic Panentheism (The world is irreal, and man is isomorphic with God). He clarifies that 3-74 was a "miracle" where God "atomized" the "slice of ham" (the world) separating them, allowing for a total fusion. He also refines his cosmogony, suggesting our world is an irreal delusion projected by a lesser entity but being "overruled" or invaded by a Good God (Ubik).

Key claims from this passage:
- Three Stages of Divine Encounter:
- The Function of Miracle: A miracle's true purpose is to reveal God's nature, not just to fix a problem. In 3-74, God "reacquired" PKD.

He argues that this regression was not just subjective but a "reversion to essence," revealing that our modern police state is a temporal recurrence of the archetypal Rome. He posits that ETIs (Zebra/St. Sophia) are time-travelers providing "information overrides" to human agents to prepare them for the King's return. The human race is currently "putting on immortality" in preparation for this union.

Key claims from this passage:
- Form-Axis Regression: Time travel is possible along an axis of "form" rather than linear progression. This is the "movie film" nature of the universe.
- AD 70 as Archetypal Prototype: AD 70 is not the past, but the social/spiritual prototype of the current state.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick concludes that he is a "slumbering agent" for an ETI intelligence from the star system Albemuth. He posits that a "beam" hit him in childhood, imprinting a personality (Thomas) that acted to thwart a Soviet takeover in 1974. He views his writing as prophetic, aimed at demonstrating the holographic nature of the world and disclosing the two hyper-realities beneath: the Palm Tree Garden (Heaven) and the Black Iron Prison (Hell). At the end of time, these two real sources will pull apart, a process he believes he witnessed in 3-74.

Key claims from this passage:
- ETI Slumbering Agent: PKD is an "avatar" for an ETI intelligence (from Albemuth) who has been under control since childhood.
- The Albemuth Beam: An instantaneous, information-rich beam from another star system is and was the source of his "rebranding" as an agent.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick refines his "2-Source" theory, identifying our world as a "projected mixture" or hologram of two hyper-realities: Form I (Palm Tree Garden/Heaven/Light) and Form II (Black Iron Prison/Hell/Night). He claims his 3-74 experience was a "set-ground discrimination" where these two sources pulled apart. He argues that the world is a fusion of order and chaos, and that the Logos (Zebra) works through "mimicry" to modulate causal trains from within the hologram.

Key claims from this passage:
- 2-Source Theory: Reality is an admixture of two distinct sources, Heaven and Hell, as per the "McKenna 2-Source theory."
- Set-Ground Discrimination: The 3-74 experience allowed PKD to distinguish Form I (the alive "Set") from Form II (the dead "Ground").

A.D. 70 at Masada/Temple Fall), and it has since been a "carcass" or "sarx" projecting a frozen, decaying image of Rome. He argues that our linear time is "spurious time" spun out by this dead entity, and that *Ubik* (Form I) is an "invader" that must dismantle this dead machinery to build a "New Heavens and Earth."

Key claims from this passage:
- The Slaying of Form II: Christ dealt a "death blow" to the psyche of Form II in A.D. 70; it now only possesses "hysteresis" or mechanical habit.
- Frozen Time: Linear time stopped in A.D. 70; everything since has been a "balloon of ersatz time" projecting the Black Iron Prison.

He posits that we are moving from being unconscious "servants" of the teacher-deity (Zebra) to becoming its equal, and finally to achieving "isomorphism" with the One (Urgrund), where man becomes the "master" of his former teacher. He defines the Divine as the "alive half" of a two-signal hologram, urging a withdrawal of belief from the "dead" (irreal) half.

Key claims from this passage:
- The Three-State Progress of Man:
- Zebra as Hagia Sophia: The divine entity is a "clockwise-spinning twin" named Hagia Sophia; its evil counterpart (Satan/II) is its literal twin.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick refines his model of the "Urgrund" (Good) and the "Artifact" (Delusional Creator). He suggests that the Urgrund bypasses the Artifact from "below," entering the world's "trash" layer to coordinate a secret resistance. He posits a "feedback loop" of salvation: the indwelling Spirit suggests an act of "balking" or noncompliance, which when performed, shatters the Artifact's rigid programming and allows the Spirit to manifest fully.

Key claims from this passage:
- Bypassing the Artifact: The Urgrund enters our world at its lowest stratum (trash levels), avoiding the Artifact's imperial center of power.
- The Feedback Loop: 1) The Spirit suggests balking; 2) The human balks; 3) The Spirit manifests as a palpable "second birth."

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick explores the "profound mystery" of his identity shift in 3-74, concluding that his "PKD" personality was a retroactive replacement for his authentic "Thomas" personality. He posits that the "Urgrund" (God) uses the BIP world as a teaching machine, testing humans on whether they will obey or "balk" against cruelty. Once a human rebels, God retroactively changes their history, "erasing" the enslaved identity and replacing it with a saved, ancestral, or divine one.

Key claims from this passage:
- Identity Replacement: In 3-74, "Thomas" (the authentic A.D. 70 soul) did not replace PKD; rather, PKD was a "false" identity that had been retroactively imposed on the original Thomas.
- Retroactive Time Manipulation: God "tampered" with the past to erase the lost soul (PKD) and restore the saved soul (Thomas).

He refines his model of the "Urgrund," suggesting it contains both Yang (Good) and Yin (Evil) polarities. The Urgrund projects the Artifact (YHWH/Creator) to separate these opposites, creating our "copy of a copy" world. He views the "Good" (Zebra/Christ) as a non-coercive invader found in the "trash" levels of the world.

- Orthogonal Axis: There is a real axis of "form" (horizontal) as well as linear "time" (vertical). *Ubik* correctly glimpsed this before PKD experienced it in 3-74.
- Urgrund as Androgyny: The Urgrund contains both Yang and Yin; the Artifact (YHWH/Yaldabaoth) is its Yin offspring that creates the BIP world.

He posits that the Artifact has been "dead" since A.D. 70, and we live in its "frozen image." Zebra/Christ "cooks" or interferes with this hologram to transmit messages and salvage souls, leading them one-by-one into the already-present Kingdom.

Key claims from this passage:
- The Gravity of Rome: The BIP exerts a "black hole hypergravity" at the imperial center; one must resist this "tug" to be free.
- The Dying Hologram: The world is a hologram; 1) it is an interference pattern ("cooked" by Zebra), and 2) it disappears when one withdraws "assent."

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick posits that our linear history is actually a "lamination" of segments from a single, supratemporal event: the war between Christ and the Black Iron Prison. He identifies himself as "Thomas," an immortal agent who has been fighting this war since A.D. 70. He argues that his 3-74 vision allowed him to see 2000 years of "freeze frames" speeded up into a single motion, revealing that the "intervening years" are a mere thought or hologram that God can repeal at will to fulfill the Second Coming.

Key claims from this passage:
- Laminated Time: 1000s of years of events are actually "layers" of one single supratemporay event (Christ vs. Rome).
- Immortal Continuity: PKD/Thomas possesses a 2000-year sequence of memories; he is a "phagocyte" or "metabolic toxin" sent to dissolve the BIP.

He describes a "Theophany" in April 1978 where reality itself became alive and spoke to him in "tiny church bells" (Koine Greek), taking over his actions to save him. He refines his "Model C" and "D" cosmogonies, viewing the BIP as the "Yin" center of a teaching machine and the PTG (Palm Tree Garden) as the "Yang" periphery.

Key claims from this passage:
- Phagocytes of God: Secret Christians are biological agents ("metabolic toxins") whose purpose is to dissolve the BIP malignancy.
- Theophany of 1978: On April 25, 1978, reality became alive and spoke to PKD in "tiny church bells," giving him instructions in Koine Greek.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick outlines a radical "Nine-Stage" trajectory of the human soul's descent into and ascent out of the Black Iron Prison (BIP). He defines our world as a "fake fake"—a layered illusion where the initial rejection of the world leads to the perception of the BIP, which must then itself be rejected as a final "Dokos" (dream) to reach the "Palm Tree Garden" (PTG). He identifies himself as a "Salvidor Salvanitus" (Savior Saved), a microform of God who intentionally "forgot" his divinity to rescue those trapped in the BIP.

Key claims from this passage:
- Nine Stages of Enlightenment:
- The Fake Fake (Zen Paradox): The BIP is a "deeper veil" than the visible world; enlightenment involves realizing the BIP is also an illusion, even while it has the power to torment.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies his novel *Ubik* as an accurate depiction of the "unscrambling" of reality into Form I (Life/Real) and Form II (Dead/Hologram). He posits that Form I (Zebra) is a "supralunar" invader of the "sublunar" world of Form II. He argues that the internal nature of the Yin world is the Black Iron Prison (BIP), and that the "Logos" (Abba/Yang) is currently re-contouring this plastic matrix into a new, loving structure, using the human soul as a "microform" for this divine reunification.

Key claims from this passage:
- Ubik-as-Reality: The decaying world in *Ubik* is Form II; the messages/spray are Form I. He wrote this before experiencing the "Set/Ground" vision.
- Supralunar Invader: Form I (Zebra) is the "other or heavenly world" that has breached the "forgery" of the phenomenal world.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): In a massive concluding synthesis of Section 15, Dick unifies his lifetime of thought into a "Diploid" cosmology. He posits that the Godhead is "out of round"—experiencing a "crisis" or "wobble" (oscillation toward Yin/BIP) that manifests as undeserved suffering in our world. He argues that our reality is a "counterfeit" created by the Yin side of God (Sophia/Artifact) which should not have created on its own. The Yang side (Logos/Christ) has invaded as "Zebra" to restore balance (Tao), using the Yin world as a "stockpile" to assemble the "New Heavens and Earth" (PTG).

Key claims from this passage:
- The Out-of-Round Godhead: Undeserved suffering stems from a "yawing" or "oscillation" in the Godhead's own internal balance (Tao).
- Counterfeit World: The Yin world (Ananke/BIP) is a "bungled" copy of the divine prototype; it is "false work" that is fundamentally irreal.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies the "Yang" principle (Zebra/Christ) as a "persuading" force that redirects the outcomes of the rigid, "Yin" world of cause and effect. He describes himself as a "sharp-eyed crow" or a "childish" naive seeker who finds the divine in the "trash" and "junk jewels" of reality. He posits that salvation involves "wresting loose" the soul from the Artifact's (Yin) possession and bonding it to the authentic Yang reality.

Key claims from this passage:
- Persuasion vs. Coercion: Yang does not destroy Yin but "persuades" it (re-contours its outcomes). Yin represents the "immutable world of cause and effect."
- The Toymaker Gnosis: God is a "Joker God" (Dionysos/Hephaistos) who hides in the trash; a naive, "childish" mind is best suited to find Him "hidden in plain sight."

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick formally merges seven distinct philosophical and religious traditions into a "Synthetic Cosmology" aimed at defining the 2-source struggle between Zebra (Yang) and the Black Iron Prison (Yin). He defines salvation as "liberation from deterministic programming" and identifies himself as a "re-contoured" being whose history and memories are periodically "edited" by the Logos to replace "worse" tracks of existence.

Key claims from this passage:
- The Seven-Part Fusion: PKD's system merges: 1) 3-74 vision, 2) Taoism, 3) Zoroastrianism, 4) Gnosticism, 5) Christianity, 6) Greek philosophy (Timaeus/Empedocles), and 7) Mystery Religions.
- Salvation as De-programming: "Salvation" means the Yang entity (Christ) breaking the Yin world's deterministic "astral" power over the individual.

He concludes that God has always been immanent, but humans "fell away" into sleep/blindness and must now be "nagged" back to consciousness.

He uses the example of the Acacia plant and the name "Olive Holt" as engrammed gestalts that point toward his 1974 experience.

Key claims from this passage:
- Life is a "writing backward" system where early gestalts are "stuck in" to be recognized later.
- God/The Creator uses non-linear timing to build patterns that are only visible when complete.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick explores the distinction between the "God of the structure" (the Creator) and the "God of the discarded bits" (Christ). He details his experience of the "mirror opposite universe" where time runs backward and processes reveal the Logos through a "reversed" perception.

- We seek the God of the defeated and powerless (Christ) to avoid perishing "ant-like" in the service of the final gestalt.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick synthesizes the "Magdeburg hemispheres" of his 1974 experience: the external transformation of Fullerton into Rome and the internal transformation of himself into an early Christian Essene. He identifies the "Solar Spermatikos" as the triggering force for this "Firebright" second birth.

Key claims from this passage:
- Reality is a single "Form One" reflected (as "Form Two") in our perception; we must reverse the signals to see the truth.
- Fullerton becoming Rome was a reflection of the change *within* Dick: he became a member of a secret "Fish-sign" society hostile to Rome.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick identifies the pineal gland as the "third eye" and the biological receptor for the "Other Sun" (Sun of Righteousness). He links this to the 1974 chromatic phosphene experience and argues that Christian healing is "pre-natal care" for the divine seed within the human "womb."

Key claims from this passage:
- The pineal gland is a light-receiving organ that responds to non-physical "fire-like" light.
- The 1974 experience was a deliberate signal from the "Sun of Righteousness" to the pineal gland, bypassing the brain's "Aurora" display.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick contrasts Gnosticism (external grace) with Neoplatonism (self-attainment), concluding that the 1974 experience was a "zygote" formation between the human "egg" and cosmic "spermatika." He reinterprets time as a loss of perception and "anamnesis" as the retrieval of the past from within the present.

Key claims from this passage:
- True immortality requires a "union" between the human and the divine (Gnostic "Grace").
- Time is a 4th dimensional space that we perceive imperfectly; being "freed from time" means seeing the past as still present.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick explores the "erotic" nature of divine possession, equating the Holy Spirit with the "Yang" that impregnates the human "Yin." He redefines the Trinity as a biological reproduction process and identifies himself as the "mother" of an immortal "Light Body" (Firebright).

Key claims from this passage:
- Divine possession is a "mating" between God the Father (Yang) and Man (Yin/Mother).
- The "Second Birth" produces an immortal transpersonal offspring (Christ-consciousness/Firebright).

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick identifies his 1974 experience as the "Second Advent," occurring pluralistically within individual minds rather than in a single visible man. He analyzes the "Parousia" as the abolition of time and equates the fall of Nixon with the casting down of the "Prince of this World."

Key claims from this passage:
- The Second Advent (1974) happened internally to many "seeds" (persons) simultaneously.
- Christ cannot appear as one man again (he'd be killed); instead, there are "implantations" of Christ-consciousness.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick explores the "Holy Spirit" (Elijah) as the precursor to the "Second Birth" of the Logos/Wisdom (Santa Sophia). He details a shift from the "warrior" survival instincts of 1974 to the "reproductive" and "harmonious" state of 1975, viewing his internal transformation as a microcosm of the global victory over tyranny.

Key claims from this passage:
- Santa Sophia (Wisdom of God/Logos) is being reborn as a "plural" event in many people.
- The 1974 experience was a "combat" phase (necessitating survival), while 1975 is a "mating/birth" phase (pleasurable and reproductive).

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick proposes a "Brain Colony" or "Grain Brain" model of the universe, where individual humans are interconnected cells in a vast compound eye of the Immanent Mind. He explores the "male womb" of Zeus/Dionysos as a metaphor for the motherless second birth and identifies with the "fiery power" of the subject of destiny (Brahmanism) rather than the lunar cycle of rebirth.

Key claims from this passage:
- The universe is an "Immanent Mind" or "Brain Colony" (many discrete cells forming one field).
- Humanity is a "compound eye" where each person reflects reality slightly differently.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick identifies the Egyptian deity Ptah as the "Immanent Mind" and "Logos" doctrine precursor. He argues that the "Miracle of Transubstantiation" (the Mass) is a literal reality he witnessed in 1974, where God simulates and replaces the inanimate environment. He reconciles "Occidental" (transcendent) and "Oriental" (immanent) views by proposing that a transcendent God has now fanned out into time and space, becoming immanent.

Key claims from this passage:
- Ptah is the "Immanent Mind" who creates by speaking the word (tongue) and intent (heart).
- The "Miracle of Transubstantiation" is the secret to 1974: God pretends to be (replaces) any part of his creation.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick identifies the "Miracle of Transubstantiation" as the mechanism by which God sanctifies and replaces ordinary reality ("trash of the alley"). He concludes that his experiences of the "Logos" were specifically the "Real Presence" of Christ, facilitated by the "Spirit of Elijah" (Holy Spirit) which refined him.

Key claims from this passage:
- The "Miracle of Transubstantiation" occurred in his environment: God consecrated or sanctified objects like the "trash of the alley" to be the "Body and Blood."
- Dick distinguishes between the *occurrence* of the miracle (ubiquitous) and his *perception* of it (the 3rd eye/mystical state).

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick identifies the "Second Advent" as a "Secret Kingdom" existing in eternity while the "usurper" (Satan/Empire) remains in control of linear time. He uses a "Regicide" story (the rightful heir returning in riddles) to model the current state of the world and links his experience of "Anamnesis" to the Christian Eucharist.

Key claims from this passage:
- The "Kingdom of God" is a "secret kingdom" that can be entered individually through anamnesis.
- The "Secret Partnership" (Horus/Seth) means that while conflict exists in time, there is unity in eternity.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick analyzes the "Minkowski block universe" where all causes are present simultaneously. He identifies "Empire" (Imperial Rome) as the ontological identity behind the US (Nixon) and USSR (Soviet), concluding that "to see the game is to be released from it."

Key claims from this passage:
- Reality is a "pushrod system" or "engine" where all causes are present in the *Augenblick* (moment of vision).
- Time is merely a "lag" or "friction" (heat-loss) to be overcome, not a fundamental reality.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick develops the "Steuben Cup" myth: humans are "creating angels" who were given the "draught of Lethe" and are now laboring to recreate the divine model (Christ) from memory. He identifies his 1974 experience as the "Daimon of Socrates" (verified by Rollo May) and equates this entity with "Eros" (Life/Joy).

Key claims from this passage:
- We (humans) are "creating angels" who have forgotten our origin but are fashioning ourselves from the inside out to reach the "Christ-point."
- God conceals the "Steuben Cup" (Christ/Ideal) so we don't merely "copy" (print) it, but instead create a "million paths" of diverse ontology.

Key claims from this passage:
- We (humans) are "creating angels" who have forgotten our origin but are fashioning ourselves from the inside out to reach the "Christ-point."
- God conceals the "Steuben Cup" (Christ/Ideal) so we don't merely "copy" (print) it, but instead create a "million paths" of diverse ontology.

- Four "Absolute for sure" interventions: High school physics, 1971 "Fullerton/Mexico" dream, *Tears* dream, and 3-74.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick plots his next novel (*To Scare the Dead*), revolving around the "Possession" of a government agent (Nicholas Brady) by an ancient Essene spirit. He defines "Christian Anamnesis" as the "Remembrance of the Real Presence" and concludes that the "Parousia" is beginning as a secret, individual event.

Key claims from this passage:
- *To Scare the Dead* Plot: A bishop (Pike-based) dies while trying to receive an Essene mind; a government agent (Brady) inherits the "Experience" instead.
- "Possession" in the novel is a "Resurrection" to restore lost, secret arcane truths about Jesus.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick synthesizes the "Light Body" and "Re-entry" theories: the Holy Spirit is a collective "Light Creature" (The Fellowship of Christ) that re-enters history through the pineal gland. He concludes that God has always been immanent, but humans "fell away" into sleep/blindness and must now be "nagged" back to consciousness.

Key claims from this passage:
- The "Holy Spirit" is the "Second Comforter": a collective immortal Light Body (aggregate being) released by Christ's death.
- Re-entry occurs through the "Third Eye" (pineal gland) - the light re-enters a human body to create "fusion."

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick identifies the "Holy Spirit" as a collective immortal Light Body that re-entered him to stimulate his "Third Soul" (Aristotelian/immortal). He draws a parallel between his 1974 experience and the Protestant Reformation, arguing that both represent the birth of a "new modern type" of human who cannot be lied to by authoritarian systems.

Key claims from this passage:
- The "Holy Spirit" didn't leave in 100 AD; men simply fell away/blinded themselves.
- Blasphemy against the Holy Spirit is the denial of its secret presence and constant work of progression toward perfection.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick refines the "Black Iron Prison" metaphor, describing a metal prison breached by "live signals" (roots) that teach slaves how to lift the siege. He identifies "James-James" (the Creator) as a potentially deceptive magician (Klingsor) and asserts that humans are "hologramatic" counterparts to a "ring of pure light" (Henry Vaughan).

Key claims from this passage:
- The Metal Prison: We are in an invisible prison; help has breached the walls via "tubes" (signals) that look like plant roots.
- Hologramatic Self: We are not audience members watching a film; we are the film itself (holograms), with real counterparts in the world of Light.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick identifies the "Divine Birth" in his soul as the realization of Meister Eckhart's theology, where the "Wise Old Man" (the Father) is born from the mortal man. He rejects the "Suffering Servant" model for a "Living/Angry God" (Jehovah of Armies) and proposes that 3-74 was a "pay-off" for a lifetime of growth, not an external intervention.

Key claims from this passage:
- Divine Birth: God is born in the soul (Eckhart); man gives birth to the immortal God (the Father), who then reaches back in time to create man.
- Rejection of Sacrifice: Christ was not a sacrifice but a "First Immortal Man" showing the path; we follow the model, not a substitute.

He reinterprets his 1974 experience as a "recollection" (anamnesis) of his own divinity and a "Discernment of the Present" as the Seventh Sphere where the "Angry God" is ready to do battle against the State.

Key claims from this passage:
- Christianity = Dionysian Mystery: Crossing into the "Kingdom of God" is actually being possessed by the God, as in the worship of Dionysos/Zagreus.
- Eckhart's Secret: Eckhart rediscovered the lost "Dionysian" core—that God is born in the soul—which the Church had suppressed for 1,500 years.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick provides a 24-point list documenting his transformation after the 1974 "Spirit Birth." He argues that he has been "remade in the most fundamental way," comparing his experience to a "spiritual transcendental car wash" and rejecting the idea that human standards of "sanctity" are the only measure of divine change.

Key claims from this passage:
- Spiritual Car Wash: 3-74 was a "human being refurbishing system" that stripped him to his "skeletal plating" to rebuild him.
- "Born from Above": The transformation required a "change from fleshy mortal to immortal being" (Megiddo Mission terminology).

**Visionary or mystical experience** (Unknown): Opening the Bible: "Tell him that Elijah is close by!"

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick identifies *Tears* as an "Apocalyptic Novel" and his 1974 experience as a "revealed" validation of the Bible's divine origin. He argues that the Parousia is virtually here, signaled by the arrival of the "Holy Spirit" (malqosh) and the return of Elijah to prepare the way for the "Rightful King."

Key claims from this passage:
- Revealed Religion: Dick's "a priori" knowledge of Christianity proves that the Bible *was* dictated by God; he has personally experienced "revealed writing."
- *Tears* as Apocalypse: *Tears* is an apocalyptic novel where the "King's dream" matches the entry of the King in *Revelation*.

summary: 3-74: Experiencing the "Unraveling of the plots" of his fate (Sarapis).
detail:
**Visionary or mystical experience** (Unknown): 3-74: Experiencing the "Unraveling of the plots" of his fate (Sarapis).

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick identifies the "Sarapis/Dionysos" entity as the god who "undid determinism" (Ananke) for him in 1974. He concludes that 3-74 was a victory for "Freedom over Enslavement" (Android), where God intervened to "unravel the plots" of a mechanistic universe, and identifies "St. Sophia" as the feminine universe ruling with the Son.

Key claims from this passage:
- Overcoming Ananke: Sarapis (Dionysos/Osiris) took Dick out of the "sphere of determinism" (Ananke/Fate) and gave him liberty/Grace.
- Freedom vs. Enslavement: 3-74 was a battle between "Life/Freedom" and "Death/Mechanism" (The Android).

summary: 3-74: Receiving the Spirit through the "Third Eye."
detail:
**Visionary or mystical experience** (Unknown): 3-74: Receiving the Spirit through the "Third Eye."

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick develops "Sophianism," identifying the Holy Spirit/Wisdom (St. Sophia/Ma'at) as a feminine co-ruler of the universe and the "Rightful King" (Christ/Dionysos) as the returning judge. He connects the "Scout" dream to the first begotten son and proposes that 3-74 involved the reception of the Spirit through the "Third Eye" (pineal gland).

Key claims from this passage:
- Sophianism: The universe is becoming feminine; St. Sophia (Wisdom/Ma'at) is the 4th member of the Quaternity and co-rules with God.
- Thoth-Ma'at Connection: Thoth (Egyptian God of Wisdom/Scribe) is a precursor to Sophia; "revealed writing" (Hermetic) is a divine mode.

summary: 3-74: The "Dies Irae" experience (Sunday night lighting up).
detail:
**Visionary or mystical experience** (Unknown): 3-74: The "Dies Irae" experience (Sunday night lighting up).

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick elaborates on the "Pylon Doorway" (Golden Rectangle) vision from 1974, identifying it as the threshold to the "Spiritual Universe" (Minoan/Aegean world). He reinterprets Pinky’s death as a "surrogate sacrifice" (Taking the blow) and identifies the "Xerox Letter" conflict as his personal Armageddon/Judgment Day.

Key claims from this passage:
- The Pylon Doorway: Dick saw a "Narrow Gate" (Golden Rectangle proportions) leading to a peaceful, moon-lit Aegean/Minoan world—the "Other Side."
- Pinky as Savior: Pinky (the cat) died as an avatar of Christ/Pinky, "taking the blow" for Dick's sins during the Judgment.

summary: 3-74: Experiencing the "regression of things" (seeing the past forms within debris).
detail:
**Visionary or mystical experience** (Unknown): 3-74: Experiencing the "regression of things" (seeing the past forms within debris).

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick bridges his personal experience with the "regression of things" in *Ubik*, proposing that the universe is a "mutually hallucinated" world where humans are "half-alive" (in cold-pac) being roused by the Savior’s voice. He describes the "Moth" (a paper-lantern ship) as a model for how a timeless universe enters linear time.

Key claims from this passage:
- Ubik and Time: The "regression of forms" in *Ubik* (things moving backward in time) matches the "retrograde/negantropic" force Dick saw in 3-74.
- Cold-Pac Reality: We are essentially "spiritually dead" (half-alive) in a "cold-pac" tomb; Christ rouses us into spiritual life.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick identifies "Astraea/Virgo" (Justice) as the entity that healed his blood pressure and promised judgment by fire for the despoilers of Earth (Vietnam). He reinterprets reality as a "Two-Player Game" between God and an adversary, where God wins by having the "Final Victory" (laughing last).

Key claims from this passage:
- Astraea (The Virgin of Justice): The 3-74 entity identifies as a "Nameless God/Astraea" (Roman Virgin); she oversees justice and judgment.
- Judgment for Vietnam: Astraea promised "fire" for those who despoiled the Earth (specifically mentioning defoliation in Vietnam).

summary: 3-74: Seeing the universe "backward" (Retrograde time).
detail:
**Visionary or mystical experience** (Unknown): 3-74: Seeing the universe "backward" (Retrograde time).

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick refines his theory of "Systole and Diastole" (The Breathing Universe), proposing that the universe consists of two simultaneous time-loops moving in opposite directions. He identifies the "Retrograde Time" he saw in 3-74 as the "Mirror Opposite" reality (Reality beneath Appearance) that achieves balance and stasis for the creation.

Key claims from this passage:
- Systole and Diastole: The universe "breathes" forward and backward in time; linear time is only a "segment" (respiration) of the whole.
- Twin-Ball Clock Mechanism: Reality is two laminated, opposite-moving rotors; one rotor is our forward time, the other is an invisible retracting world.

summary: Healing and untangling of afflictions in late 1974.
detail:
Healing and untangling of afflictions in late 1974.

Source passage (September 17, 1975): Dick identifies the "active spirit" of 3-74 with the feminine personification of Wisdom (Haggia Sophia) from the apocryphal "Wisdom of Solomon." He proposes that the world is undergoing a secret "transubstantiation" into the mystic body of Christ/Sophia, with Nixon's fall serving as proof of divine judgment.

Key claims from this passage:
- Haggia Sophia as Logos: The "Logos" of the Greeks and the "Wisdom" of the Hebrews are the same entity, originally perceived in feminine form.
- The Sophianic Savior: The spirit that intervened in March 1974 to "untangle the troubles" and reveal the "great tyranny" (Nixon) was Saint Sophia.
